producer: demiurge MONDALOY.md §9.8 — proactive d2 breakthrough execution of the §9.7 handoff next_steps_after_this_handoff item #4 (re-run §9.6 funnel with lower Al+Ti loading for printability-wall fallback). Computed BEFORE any c1 wet-lab attempt so the next candidate is on the shelf when route B reports back.
purpose: Pre-computed fallback envelope for the c1 LPBF hot-cracking failure mode (route B in the §9.7 handoff). Re-runs the §9.6 forward funnel with the (Al=4.0, Ti=1.0) wt% delta specified verbatim in the c1 handoff JSON's next_steps_after_this_handoff item #4. Goal: have c2 ready BEFORE c1 wet-lab returns, satisfying d1 (non-wet-lab verification driven to completed-form for the next round) and d2 (proactive surfacing of the breakthrough path on the printability wall, not waiting for the wall to manifest).

boundary.what_this_record_is: A computed-projection envelope for the c2 candidate. Composition delta from c1 is fixed (handoff JSON next_steps); property re-projections use public correlations (γ′-fraction vs Al+Ti at%, Giggins-Pettit region-III gate, Kou hot-cracking susceptibility).
boundary.what_this_record_is_NOT: Not a measured-oracle record. Not a wet-lab attempt. absorbed=false permanently. provisional=true reflects that even the c2 design needs an LPBF parameter sweep + TGA confirmation before any property claim is hardened.
boundary.wet_lab_boundary: c2 will need its own wet-lab handoff packet (a future §9.x record analogous to §9.7) IF a c2 cast/print is ever commissioned. d6 absolute: any future absorbed=true flip is permitted only on an analog reference (Haynes-214 / René-N5 class), never on SX500 itself or on a c-candidate's design record.

Al_wt_pct.rationale: γ′-former cut to lower hot-crack susceptibility per §9.7 handoff JSON next_steps #4 (verbatim)

Ti_wt_pct.rationale: γ′-former cut, same lever as Al

delta_from_c1.all_other_elements: unchanged from c1 (Cr 20.0, Co 12.0, Mo 3.5, Hf 0.3, Y 0.05, C 0.05, B 0.005)

composition_at_pct_derived.note: atomic-percent re-expression for melt-shop charge calc and DFT/CALPHAD downstream input (atomic masses g/mol: Ni 58.69, Cr 52.00, Al 26.98, Co 58.93, Mo 95.96, Ti 47.87, Hf 178.49, Y 88.91, C 12.01, B 10.81). Values rounded to 0.01 at%.
composition_at_pct_derived.rounding_note: Sum closes to 100 within rounding. DFT/CALPHAD downstream should use full-precision moles, not rounded at%.

design_rules_re_applied.alumina_former_gate: Giggins-Pettit Ni-Cr-Al oxidation map (1971): Cr ~21.28 at% places the region-III (continuous α-Al2O3) Al threshold at ~8-9 at%. c2 Al = 8.20 at% sits ON the region II/III boundary — region III is probable but margin is thin (vs c1 at 10.13 at% with comfortable margin). Reactive-element ballast (Hf 0.3 + Y 0.05) held to compensate.
design_rules_re_applied.gamma_prime_printability: (Al+Ti) drops 6.5 → 5.0 wt% (9.36 at%) — γ′ volume fraction estimated 27-32 % (vs c1 35-40 %). Closer to Haynes 282 (~19 %, weldable) than to c1 — printability window OPENS, hot-crack susceptibility DROPS by an estimated 20-30 %.
design_rules_re_applied.tcp_avoidance: PHACOMP / Nv electron-vacancy: Cr 20.0 wt%, Mo 3.5 wt% UNCHANGED from c1 — still below σ-phase line. The Al/Ti cut does not affect TCP risk (Al/Ti are γ′-formers, not σ-promoters).
design_rules_re_applied.reactive_element: Hf 0.3 wt% + Y 0.05 wt% held — reactive-element-effect coverage critical given the thinner Al reserve. c3a fallback path would raise Hf to 0.4 if region III fails to hold.
design_rules_re_applied.co_solvus: Co 12.0 wt% held — γ′ solvus retention is the lever that prevents service-temperature loss despite reduced γ′ fraction.

estimated_properties.oxidation_regime: α-Al2O3 expected (region III, threshold-grazing) — k_p estimated SAME ORDER as c1 ~1e-13 to 1e-12 g^2 cm^-4 s^-1 at ~1100 C BUT skewing to the HIGH END of the band (thinner Al reserve consumed faster). If region III does NOT hold, c2 reverts to region II (Cr2O3 + internal Al2O3) → CrO3 volatilisation risk in ox-rich service → c3a fallback path activates.
estimated_properties.density: ~8.32 g/cm^3 (negligible change from c1 ~8.30; Ni rebalance dominates)
estimated_properties.hot_cracking_susceptibility_qualitative: estimated -20 to -30 % vs c1 (Kou |dT/dfs^0.5| scales with γ′-former content; lower Al+Ti narrows the γ-γ′ eutectic freezing range). This is the c2 PURPOSE — buy printability margin.
estimated_properties.creep_strength_qualitative: estimated -10 to -20 % vs c1 (γ′ Orowan strengthening scales with fraction). Trade is explicit and accepted.
estimated_properties.n6_principal_elements: Ni · Cr · Al · Co · Mo · Ti (unchanged from c1 — element set identical, only loadings shifted)

ascii_giggins_pettit_locator: | /     ||  (boundary line ~ Al 8-9 at% at Cr 22 at%)

d2_fallback_paths_for_c2.name: c3 envelopes (named, not pre-computed) — d2 forward path if c2 also hits a wall

c3a_region_III_recovery.trigger: c2 oxidation kinetics show region-II Cr2O3 break-in rather than continuous α-Al2O3 (the threshold-grazing failure mode)
c3a_region_III_recovery.delta_from_c2: Al 4.0 → 4.5 wt%, Hf 0.3 → 0.4 wt% (extra reactive-element ballast to compensate for thinner Al reserve)
c3a_region_III_recovery.γ′_window: ~30 % (lower end of c1's window, upper end of c2's)
c3a_region_III_recovery.tradeoff: halfway between c1 and c2 on the printability axis

c3b_co_driven_strength_recovery.trigger: c2 prints fine but creep/strength drops too far (γ′ 27-32 % vs c1 35-40 %)
c3b_co_driven_strength_recovery.delta_from_c2: Co 12 → 14 wt% (lower SFE → planar slip → strengthening independent of γ′), add 1 wt% W (solid-solution)
c3b_co_driven_strength_recovery.tradeoff: recovers high-T strength without touching γ′-former pool — orthogonal lever

d2_fallback_paths_for_c2.boundary: Both c3 paths stay strictly inside the §9.4 reverse-engineered envelope. Neither pre-computed here — named so the c2 wet-lab report has a forward path on the shelf the moment it lands.

honest_observations: c2 is NOT 'better than c1' — it trades strengthening-phase content (γ′ -20 to -25 %) for printability margin (HCS -20 to -30 %). d7 invariant: no target-forcing, the trade is reported as it falls out of public correlations.
c1 remains the PRIMARY candidate; c2 is the FALLBACK staged for the LPBF hot-cracking failure mode of c1 route B.

Giggins-Pettit margin is the c2 risk: Al 8.20 at% sits ON the region II/III boundary line at Cr ~22 at% — region III is probable, not guaranteed. If wet-lab measurement (TGA + SEM-EDS cross-section) shows Cr2O3-dominant scale instead of α-Al2O3, c3a is the named recovery path (not concession — d2 invariant).

Both routes A and B from the §9.7 handoff remain applicable to c2 with no protocol-stub modification (same VIM melt, same investment cast OR LPBF, same heat-treatment cycle — only the charge wt% changes).

absorbed=false / provisional=true / simulation-only-prediction — this is a fallback envelope, not a measurement. No d6 violation; no SX500 claim. SX500's absorbed stays false permanently in demiurge regardless of how many c-candidates the funnel emits.

d1 is satisfied: the proactive c2 execution means when c1 wet-lab returns 'hot-cracked', the next funnel pass is ALREADY DONE — non-wet-lab verification driven to completed-form for this round, not deferred to the post-failure session.
next_steps_after_this_envelope: If c1 wet-lab succeeds (route A or B): c2 stays on the shelf as a documented alternative — no immediate action needed.
If c1 route B hot-cracks: this c2 envelope is the input to a new §9.x wet-lab handoff packet (analogous to §9.7 but with c2 composition). Same VIM/LPBF protocols apply.
If c1 route A succeeds but route B cracks: c2 specifically targets the LPBF wall — promote c2 to a §9.x LPBF-only handoff, keep c1 as the cast-route reference.

Non-wet-lab follow-ups (applicable to BOTH c1 and c2): pycalphad + open Ni-Cr-Al-Co TDB phase-equilibrium run (numerical γ′ fraction confirmation — would distinguish c1 35-40 % from c2 27-32 % rigorously); DFT α-Al2O3 / Ni-substrate adhesion-energy check (region-III margin numerical, critical for c2's boundary position); physics-descriptor ML creep estimate (arxiv:2212.06755) on both candidates side-by-side.
If c2 also hits a wall (region II break-in OR creep deficit), execute c3a or c3b (named above) — d2 forward path, never concede.
r4_invariant: absorbed=false · gate_type=simulation-only-prediction · provisional=true · domain=material · verb=design. d6 absolute (SX500 absorbed=false permanent; c2 also computed-projection, absorbed=false; analog measurements would be filed as SEPARATE records and only those records could ever flip absorbed=true). d7 preserved (no target-forcing — c2's γ′ fraction reported as the correlation gives it, NOT pushed to match c1; trade-offs surfaced explicitly: γ′ -20 to -25 %, HCS -20 to -30 %). d2 preserved (proactive breakthrough EXECUTION of the c1 handoff fallback path, not waiting for wet-lab failure; c3a/c3b further breakthrough paths named for c2 walls, no concession). d1 preserved (non-wet-lab verification driven to completed-form for this round — c2 ready before c1 returns). D1 clean-room satisfied (composition delta from the c1 handoff JSON public next_steps; no proprietary SX500 process).
citations: MONDALOY.md §9.6 (forward funnel design rules) + §9.7 (c1 handoff, next_steps_after_this_handoff item #4 — c2 composition delta source) + §9.8 (this envelope's spec home)
Giggins, C.S. & Pettit, F.S. 1971 J. Electrochem. Soc. 118:1782 — Ni-Cr-Al oxidation map (region II/III boundary)
Reed, R.C. 'The Superalloys: Fundamentals and Applications' Cambridge UP 2006 — γ′ fraction vs Al+Ti correlation, PHACOMP, reactive-element-effect
Kou, S. 2015 Welding J. 94:374s — hot-cracking susceptibility criterion |dT/dfs^0.5| (Kou cracking index)
Haynes 282 datasheet — γ′ ~19 % weldable reference (lower-γ′ end of the printable Ni-superalloy bracket)
René N5 datasheet — γ′ ~70 % single-crystal reference (upper-γ′ end, NOT printable)
